quote_of_day.text:
>>57956
As much as i hated it, I am nostalgic for it. There were actually non-retarded anons back there, who could engage in discussions. The man was clearly doing something right.
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of sports disappointment moved over the board this morning, as Anon lamented Argentina's loss. The discussion remained light, with a high of 103 posts.

A scattered drizzle of personal confessions occurred in the afternoon, as one Anon shared details of losing their virginity and invited questions. This thread drew a moderate 48 posts.

Nearby, a front of nationalist sentiment blew in, with Anon questioning why Indian women weren't wearing Indian flag bikinis. This thread garnered 42 posts.

A cold snap of nostalgia touched the board late in the day, as Anon expressed longing for the old board. A related thread about router 'jeets' being unremarkable also circulated, each drawing about 30 posts.

articles.title: Anon Laments Argentina's Loss
articles.body:
Anon opened the thread with a simple 'Mufa anulo', sparking discussion on a recent football match. Anon claimed Argentina received a yellow card, and suggested England was trying to influence the referee into being overly impartial, potentially leading to Argentina getting more severe warnings.

Another anon countered that Argentina committed multiple deserving tackles and attacks before receiving their first yellow card. Anon expressed frustration, stating 'Its over' and mentioning 'argi fatigue'.

They predicted a 'slurp fest' in an upcoming Spain match and joked about a potential win for Riggi. One anon argued England gave up football and 'parked the bus' after 25 minutes, deserving to lose.

Anon shared a link to a video, while another anon attributed Argentina's win to not giving up hope after England's first goal. Anon also claimed Argentina's goals were strategic, whereas England's first goal was 'kind of lucked out'.

Some anons criticized refereeing, with one stating there were 12 fouls by Argentina and only one yellow card. The discussion included jabs at various teams and players, with anons using terms like 'Vargentina' and 'Riggi'.

Anon vector made a prediction, stating England would win and 'lassi's fam will cry hard today'. Towards the end, anons continued to discuss the match and its implications, with one saying 'Ronaldo bros, what do we do now? We keep getting cucked'.

articles.title: Anon Loses Virginity, AMA
articles.body:
Anon posted an AMA about losing their virginity. The original poster simply stated they lost their virginity the previous night and invited questions. One respondent immediately questioned the post's authenticity, suggesting it was either a timestamp or a LARP.

Another anon asked about the person's current and expected CTC, which seemed unrelated to the topic of losing one's virginity. Some anons made comments about body hair.

One suggested the person should shave, while another mentioned that unshaved areas are disgusting. One anon jokingly told the OP to tell 'her' to shave, and also humorously suggested the OP shave their hands too.

The thread took a somewhat confusing turn with these tangents, but the OP did not provide further details about their experience.

articles.title: Anon questions why Indian women aren't wearing Indian flag bikinis
articles.body:
Anon started a discussion by saying that only Indian women are not allowed to wear Indian flag bikinis and questioned why women aren't raising their voices about this issue.

One respondent said that the color combination of the Indian flag, green and saffron, would look odd on a bikini, and another added that it would look particularly unattractive on a "gobar" - a derogatory term for a cow.

Another anon countered that people are just not used to seeing the Indian flag on a bikini, comparing it to seeing American or other flags used in a similar way.

One more anon suggested that if a "sexually attractive white woman" wore a tricolored bikini, it might actually work.

Some anons expressed that the Indian flag and national symbols are sanctified in their culture, which is why it's not common to see them used on clothing like bikinis.

Others made derogatory comments about those who might object to the idea, calling them "gobarjeetas" who have no love for the nation.

The conversation also devolved into personal attacks and insults, with one anon calling another a "blind faggot" and another asking for moderator intervention.

articles.title: Anon claims router 'jeets' are unremarkable
articles.body: Anon started a thread saying 'Router jeets could never.' One respondent asked, 'What do you do with it.' Another anon said people with fast routers are 'useless' and do not use their speed for anything worthwhile, adding that '90% of internet is slop anyways.' A separate poster called someone a derogatory name. Anon also dismissed fast routers as a 'waste of extra money' unless used to full potential.

articles.title: Anon expresses nostalgia for old board
articles.body: Anon posted a brief comment saying an image was "kekworthy". Another anon replied, expressing nostalgia for an old board, saying there used to be "non-retarded anons" who could engage in discussions. One anon joked about posting something on a different board, saying it would get them banned. Anon responded with a laugh, "kek".
